news.headline: When Nationalism Beats Nationalism
news.summary: A progressive, pluralistic nationalism has prevailed at the polls in Scotland and Wales. Could it gain traction elsewhere?

news.headline: Southeast Asia’s climate; Singaporeans and a work ethic stereotype: 7 Asia highlights
news.summary:

news.headline: Hong Kong set to benefit as Xi-Trump summit signals ‘more stability’, John Lee says
news.summary:
Hong Kong’s economy and trade with the United States will benefit from greater stability and certainty brought by the summit between Chinese President Xi Jinping and his US counterpart Donald Trump, the city’s leader has said.

news.headline: Tasmanian government apologises over stolen body parts scandal
news.summary: An investigation found that 177 human specimens from dozens of bodies were secretly kept by a museum.

news.headline: Marcos says Philippines, because of proximity to Taiwan, likely involved in any conflict
news.summary:
The Philippines is likely to be involved in any conflict over Taiwan because of its proximity and the nearly 200,000 Filipinos working there, President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr told Japanese media on Monday, ahead of a state visit to Japan next week.

news.headline: How Philadelphia’s Democratic primary tests the bounds of US progressivism
news.summary: The race for Pennsylvania's third congressional district reveals divisions between Democrats closely aligned on policy.

news.headline: Trump’s Portrait Pops Up on Rickshaws in India to Mark America’s 250th
news.summary: The president is popping up on the back of the iconic three-wheeler to kick off America’s 250th anniversary celebrations in India.
